description: Lifecycle case (mobile release plan) with a reversed decision and a ruled-out/deprecated item; the chat contains the superseded content, the live state does not.

chat.content: Plan for shipping the new sync feature on mobile?
Ship it with the feature flag defaulted ON for all users in the next release.
The flag-on build crashed for ~8% of testers on launch.
Then reverse it: the decision is to default the flag OFF and enable gradually. Default-on is dropped.
Do we still support iOS 14 for this?
No — iOS 14 support is deprecated for the sync feature; minimum is iOS 15. The iOS-14 plan is replaced.
What's the confirmed cause of the crash?
A threading bug: the sync writes to the DB off the main actor, causing a race. That's established.
Conflict: product wants a 50% rollout next week, QA wants 10% for two weeks first. Open.

The exact staged-rollout percentages beyond the first step — undecided. So: flag defaults OFF (gradual enable), iOS 15 minimum, crash is a threading/race bug, rollout-speed conflict and staged-percentage question open.
